thoughts.subject: Assessing Capabilities Now
thoughts.description: I've registered the user's ASCII art query. Affirmative response is the play, possibly with a demo. However, my core function dictates brevity. I'm prioritizing the primary software engineering directive.

content.text: yes can you maybe make a cute robot like claude code's one but unique?

messages.content:
I've designed a few unique, cute robots for you. Here are three options:

**Option 1: The Explorer**
```text
      / \
     | o |
    /|___|\
   / |   | \
  |  |___|  |
      | |
     /   \
```

**Option 2: The Round Bot**
```text
     .---.
    / @ @ \
   (  \_/  )
    ) --- (
   /       \
   | [   ] |
   '-------'
```

**Option 3: The Antenna Bot**
```text
      ___
     |_|_|
    ( ^_^ )
    /|___|\
   |_|   |_|
     |   |
     '---'
```

Do any of these catch your eye, or should I try another style? I can also save your favorite to a file if you'd like.

thoughts.subject: Envisioning ASCII Robot Art
thoughts.description: I'm starting to sketch out some robot designs in my head. Thinking about variations, maybe some with different antenna or arm styles to make them stand out. It's a bit of a challenge to get the right balance of cute and distinct with these ASCII characters, but I'm confident I can come up with something original.

thoughts.subject: Constructing Robot Art Options
thoughts.description: I've got three distinct robot designs sketched out now: a round-headed version, a minimalist option, and one inspired by Gemini. The challenge now is refining these. I am considering different eye styles and body shapes to make them even more unique. I can then present them as ASCII art in the chat window, ready for the user's feedback.

content.text: i mean like highly detailed one maybe? like those https://asciigen.art/#animations

messages.content:
Here is a more detailed, unique robot designed in ASCII. I've focused on using a mix of characters to give it depth and a friendly, "Gemini CLI" personality.

```text
                _
               (_)
               | |
               | |
              _|_|_
             |     |
          ___|_____|___
         /             \
        |  ___________  |

        | [     ^     ] |
        | [  \_____/  ] |
        |  ___________  |
        |               |
        \_______________/
           |         |
      _____|_________|_____
     /                     \
    /   _________________   \
   /   /                 \   \
  |   |   G E M I N I     |   |
  |   |       C L I       |   |
  |   \_________________/   |
  |                         |
  \_________________________/
          |         |
          |         |
       ___|___   ___|___
      /       \ /       \
      |_______| |_______|
```

Does this high-detail version work for you, or would you like me to try a different "robot type" (like a spider-bot, a floating drone, etc.)?

thoughts.subject: Designing a Robot
thoughts.description: I'm currently focused on constructing a highly detailed ASCII art robot. My aim is to go beyond a basic design, drawing inspiration from "cute" robots while ensuring a unique aesthetic, much like Claude's code. I'm prioritizing intricate details to achieve a visually rich output.

I will also make some changes to the existing ASCII art to match the instructions.
